violations.detail: Back contains ', often introducing insertions or deletions' — the ', introducing' pattern is explicitly listed as a back-scan violation. Two facts are joined: (1) NHEJ is error-prone, (2) it introduces insertions or deletions. Must be split.

violations.rule: Back brevity / atomicity: 'and' joining two actions
violations.detail: Back is 16 words ('It is a required recognition site adjacent to the target for Cas9 to bind and cut') — well over the 10-word guideline. Contains 'and' joining two distinct actions (bind and cut), plus positional info packed in. Should be split.

violations.rule: No enumerations, even disguised as cloze
violations.detail: Two independent facts (NHEJ and HDR) blanked with the same {{c1}} marker. This is a 2-item cloze enumeration — the exact pattern SKILL.md prohibits. Each fill is an independent repair pathway name.

violations.rule: Back scan: semicolon joins two independent facts
violations.detail: Cloze text contains ';' joining two independent clauses (NHEJ for disruption; HDR for insertion). The back-scan rule states every ';' is a bug.

NHEJ/HDR is literally the card selection guide's canonical example of a mirror-deducible pair: 'NHEJ causes gene knockout + HDR enables gene insertion — if only two repair pathways exist and you know one does knockout, the other must do insertion.' Cards 6, 7, and 10 all cover this same NHEJ-vs-HDR distinction. Card 10 is a combined restatement of 6 and 7.

violations.rule: Specific constant values without understanding why
violations.detail: '~20 nucleotides' is a bare numerical constant with no companion card explaining WHY this length matters (genome specificity, off-target minimization). SKILL.md says to skip specific constant values without understanding why they matter.

At most 3/10 cards (30%) are why/how/reasoning (cards 3, 6, 8 generously). At least 6/10 (60%) are definitional/factual. SKILL.md requires converting some to 'why' or 'what happens if' cards when more than half are definitional.
Most cards share the same generic tags (biology, crispr, genetics). SKILL.md calls for subtopic-level tags (e.g., guide-rna, pam, cas9, bacterial-immunity) to enable meaningful filtering.
